subject: cds-bash script package

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

thanks

do you want that we add some linters?
@Bartek can you add them?
json?
yaml?
python?

it is a very big patch for review.
Once linters set I think the best way is to merge and ask you to do a demo during an integration weekly meeting...

It is probably a bit late for Guilin, but it would be interesting to see how to integrate some of the tests in CI.

Patch Set 1:

a README file to document the code will be also appreciated :)

Sure, https://jira.onap.org/browse/INT-1742

Patch Set 1:

At first glance it looks like this test suite could leverage Robot framework - I can see a lot of bash scripts performing requests and asserting the response - I guess it is a perfect usecase for Robot, no?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> At first glance it looks like this test suite could leverage Robot framework - I can see a lot of bash scripts performing requests and asserting the response - I guess it is a perfect usecase for Robot, no?

These suites are used and maintained as such by Bell. They used them in their env.
They are sharing them with the community in order to improve the test coverage of CDS.

So I do not see the a big interest to translate them into robot (or in postman or pythonsdk or any other framework)..
the most important is the content of the tests (the url..) not necessarily the framework used to launch them.
Using robot would bring additional built-in features and could be seen as a consistency improvement with some our existing tests (note most of the robot healtcheck tests are useless because already covered by kubernetes native readiness and liveness tests)
So if people find time to do it why not but for me it is not a top priority.

Patch Set 1:

The max code line difference should be approx 2000 lines, until unless it's all dependent.

I would say this review can be splitted in multiple reviewes (keeping different test scenarios separately)

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Just to be clear, am I correct in understanding these bash scripts would be run OUTSIDE of any docker container. My main concern is whether this would require us to install bash (licensed under LGPL v3) as part of our ONAP dockers.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

1) Look at Comment Dan posted:

Just to be clear, am I correct in understanding these bash scripts would be run OUTSIDE of any docker container. My main concern is whether this would require us to install bash (licensed under LGPL v3) as part of our ONAP dockers.

2) Is it possible to split this review in multiple chunks ?

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1
> 
> 1) Look at Comment Dan posted:
> 
> Just to be clear, am I correct in understanding these bash scripts would be run OUTSIDE of any docker container. My main concern is whether this would require us to install bash (licensed under LGPL v3) as part of our ONAP dockers.
> 
> 2) Is it possible to split this review in multiple chunks ?

it is a baseline of the CDS tests used by Bell and shared with the community
for the moment it is just a set of bash/json/yml/kt/python  scripts to be run on a CDS system (even independently from ONAP)

It is not dockerized, not packaged, not integrated in any test in CI
There is no plan for distribution for the moment, at least nothing for G release.

We need to discuss with people who submitted the patch how we can use it the most efficiently
shall write robot tests, update the cds support in pythonsdk? just run the different sh script?
shall it be done at the CSIT level (CDS standalone) or in the integration chains (ONAP context)

meanwhile I prefer to get them versioned in a repo rather than in a zip file or in the wiki.

@Santos/Sébastien, could we plan a demo during weekly integration meeting to see how you are using these tests within Bell context and see how we can leverage them for the community in H release?

Patch Set 6:

if no objection I planned to merge it as a reference baseline end of this week
it will not be integrated in any chain and will not trigger any docker build
we need to figure out how to use properly it for Honolulu

@santosh It would be good to plan a demo, how you are using it today in Bell and discuss what would be the best scenario for automation.
